site.title: WorkComp Risk Radar
site.tagline: Daily risk signals across the NSW & Australian workers compensation insurance landscape.

digests.posture: Commencement day: from 4pm today (30 June) and 1 July the NSW reform package moves from legislation to live operation, so the signal shifts from anticipating change to operating under it — new claims, premium and market-practice settings now bind insurers and agents, with late government concessions softening the day-one rules and a $344m prevention package coming online alongside.

Commencement eve in NSW: with the reform package taking effect from 4pm 30 June / 1 July, the live signals are a pre-commencement rush of claims lodged under the old rules, mounting evidence that tighter eligibility is pushing mental-injury claims into superannuation, and a step-change in psychosocial enforcement exposure.

NSW dominates the signal: the scheme's largest reform in over a decade commences 30 June – 1 July, bringing new SIRA guidelines, a frozen premium target, a new employer excess and a far tighter psychological-injury test.
